pipelineA.brief:
Thundercat — American musician, singer, producer, songwriter, and bassist — is the name I keep coming back to. According to Bandcamp Daily, Thundercat, the Los Angeles bass virtuoso, was the secret weapon of Kendrick Lamar's "To Pimp a Butterfly" and Flying Lotus's "Cosmogramma," anchoring their sprawling, chaotic brilliance. Both Bandcamp Daily[1] and Bandcamp LA Discover[2] picked this up independently. The corroboration here is real — these are not the same editors reading the same press release. This has been consistent — which at this stage is more telling than a loud entrance.

Earl Sweatshirt, MIKE & SURF GANG, “POMPEII // UTILITY” is still here. Sure, fans may rejoice that two of their favourite artists are working together, but equally, they might be fearful of a low common denominator outcome. Staying power at this stage in music is more interesting than a loud entrance.
pipelineA.title: Thundercat

According to Bandcamp Daily, Thundercat, the virtuosic bass-plucker, has been the secret weapon of the Los Angeles beat scene, anchoring Kendrick Lamar's "To Pimp a Butterfly" and Flying Lotus's "Cosmogramma." Both Bandcamp Daily[1] and Bandcamp LA Discover[2] picked this up independently. This is what real emergence looks like in music: different vantage points, same name, no coordination. Staying power at this stage matters more than a sharp spike.

Earl Sweatshirt, MIKE & SURF GANG, “POMPEII // UTILITY” is still here. Sure, fans may rejoice that two of their favourite artists are working together, but equally, they might be fearful of a low common denominator outcome. Staying power at this stage in music is more interesting than a loud entrance.
pipelineA.title: Thundercat
